description: A square 'arrest card' graphic from ICE's 'THE WORST FIRST' branded series. The background is a high-contrast black-and-white close-up photograph of a person's hand and wrist locked in a metal handcuff, with the open palm facing up. A red diagonal banner across the top-right corner reads 'NEW ORLEANS' in white serif text (a regional/field-office tag). The upper-left carries a light-blue (cyan) serif headline 'ARRESTED AND PENDING REMOVAL' with a white sub-line '2/11/2025 | New Orleans, LA'. The subject's name is set large in bold white sans-serif over the photo: 'Dany Belkis Vasquez-Rodriguez'. Below it, 'CRIMES:' in bold white with a bullet: 'Fentanyl trafficking conspiracy.' Lower-left shows the DHS seal and white 'ICE' wordmark. Lower-right is a red box containing the program logotype '-THE- WORST FIRST' in white serif type. The design uses dramatic handcuff imagery, a consistent red/black/white template, and the 'Worst First' slogan to frame the individual as a dangerous priority criminal, targeting a public social-media audience to publicize and justify removals.
notable_text: ARRESTED AND PENDING REMOVAL; 2/11/2025 | New Orleans, LA; Dany Belkis Vasquez-Rodriguez; CRIMES: Fentanyl trafficking conspiracy; THE WORST FIRST; NEW ORLEANS
